title: rubus × nothus f. sanctiformis
intro: Rubus × nothus f. sanctiformis, also known as the wild raspberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid species of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, offering antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × nothus f. sanctiformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection, as well as its traditional use in treating digestive issues and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and roots can also be dried and used as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
